questions.q: What is a relation? Define it mathematically.
questions.answer: A relation is a set of ordered pairs. Mathematically, it can be represented as R = {(a, b) | a ∈ A, b ∈ B, a ∈ D, b ∈ D}, where A and B are non-empty sets.
key_points: Order of elements in a relation

questions.q: What is an equivalence relation? Give an example.
questions.answer: An equivalence relation is a relation R on a set A such that it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. Example: Relation R on set A = {1, 2, 3} defined by R = {(1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3), (1, 2), (2, 1), (2, 3), (3, 2)} is an equivalence relation.

questions.q: What is a function? Define it mathematically.
questions.answer: A function is a relation from a non-empty set A to a non-empty set B such that every element of A has one and only one image in B. Mathematically, it can be represented as f: A → B, where for every x ∈ A, there exists a unique y ∈ B, denoted as f(x) = y.

questions.q: What is the range of a function? Give an example.
questions.answer: The range of a function f: A → B is the set of all images of elements of A under f. Example: Function f(x) = 2x + 1, defined on set A = {0, 1, 2}, has a range of {1, 3, 5}.

questions.q: What is the composition of functions? Define it mathematically.
questions.answer: The composition of two functions f: A → B and g: B → C is a function g ∘ f: A → C, defined as (g ∘ f)(x) = g(f(x)) for all x ∈ A. Mathematically, it can be represented as g ∘ f: A → C, where (g ∘ f)(x) = g(f(x)).

questions.q: What is a one-to-one function? Give an example.
questions.answer: A function f: A → B is said to be one-to-one (injective) if f(x1) = f(x2) implies x1 = x2 for all x1, x2 ∈ A. Example: Function f(x) = 2x, defined on set A = {0, 1, 2, 3}, is one-to-one.

questions.q: What is the inverse of a function? Define it mathematically.
questions.answer: The inverse of a function f: A → B, denoted as f^(-1): B → A, is a function that satisfies f(f^(-1)(y)) = y and f^(-1)(f(x)) = x for all x ∈ A and y ∈ B. Mathematically, it can be represented as f^(-1): B → A, where f^(-1)(y) = x if and only if f(x) = y.
key_points: Definition of inverse of a function
Properties of inverse of a function

questions.q: What is a many-to-one function? Give an example.
questions.answer: A function f: A → B is said to be many-to-one (not injective) if there exist distinct elements x1, x2 ∈ A such that f(x1) = f(x2). Example: Function f(x) = |x|, defined on set A = {-1, 1}, is many-to-one.

questions.q: What is the domain of a function? Give an example.
questions.answer: The domain of a function f: A → B is the set of all elements of A for which f is defined. Example: Function f(x) = 1/x, defined on set A = {0, 1, 2, 3}, has a domain of {1, 2, 3}.
questions.topic: Domain and Range of a Function

questions.q: What is the identity function? Define it mathematically.
questions.answer: The identity function on a set A, denoted as I_A: A → A, is a function defined as I_A(x) = x for all x ∈ A. Mathematically, it can be represented as I_A: A → A, where I_A(x) = x for all x ∈ A.
